articles.title: Iran Defies US Warships? Satellite Images Show 'OIL TANKERS LOADING' At Kharg; 'NOTHING CAN STOP...'
articles.description: Fresh satellite imagery is challenging U.S. claims that Iran’s oil system is collapsing under pressure. Images from Kharg Island — the backbone of Tehran’s crude exports — show multiple tankers actively loading oil, signaling that operations are still underway despite the naval blockade. According to Bloomberg, the number of vessels anchored near the island has surged, suggesting Iran has accelerated loading activity since the blockade began. The visuals directly contradict statements by U.S. President Donald Trump, who claimed Iran’s oil network was “choking.”

articles.title: ‘THOUSANDS ON STREETS’: Europe Spirals Into Chaos As Violent Clashes, Fires & Arrests Grip Capitals
articles.description: Europe’s streets are a battlefield this May Day. From Paris to Berlin, International Workers’ Day has transformed into a massive anti-government uprising. Protesters are burning effigies of Emmanuel Macron and clashing with riot police in the German capital, fueled by a toxic mix of high energy prices and anger over the U.S.-led blockade in the Gulf. As labor unions demand wage hikes to counter runaway inflation, the 2026 protests suggest a continent reaching its breaking point.

articles.title: Shilpa Rao addresses AR Rahman communal remark row: You have to abstain as much as possible from being gullible'
articles.description: Playback singer Shilpa Rao has responded to AR Rahman's comments on alleged communal influences in Bollywood. Rao emphasized the importance of artistic authenticity, stating that artists might sacrifice projects to stay true to their creative instincts. She believes choosing personal expression over safe, conventional work, even with potential failure, is crucial for self-representation.

articles.title: Trump 'not satisfied' with new Iran proposal amid ceasefire stalemate
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Friday he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal, as peace talks remain frozen despite a weeks-long ceasefire. "At this moment I'm not satisfied with what they're offering," Trump told reporters, blaming stalled talks on "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership.

articles.title: Trump calls critics of Iran war 'treasonous', says US is winning
articles.description: US President Donald Trump labelled critics of the Iran conflict 'treasonous,' asserting the US is winning. He claimed Iran's military is 'decimated' and expressed dissatisfaction with their latest proposal for a deal to end the conflict.

articles.title: Iran's IRGC announces new rules for control over Arabian Gulf waters
articles.description: Iran's IRGC announced 'new rules' to tighten control over the Arabian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz. The move aims to control 2,000 km of coastline amid escalating tensions with the US, which recently imposed a naval blockade.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Lockdown in strongroom areas after TMC, BJP clash
articles.description: Kolkata police have imposed strict prohibitory orders around EVM strongrooms following Trinamool's vote tampering claims and clashes with BJP workers. Security has been heightened with additional forces and sealed roads. The Election Commission vows to punish any disruption, emphasizing robust security at all counting centers statewide to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: 'The end of the world is on people's minds. We have the power to destroy or...' – inspiring lessons on responsibility, human power and how our choices shape the world by Oscar-winning actor of Leaving Las Vegas
articles.description: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: Nicolas Cage emphasizes that humans possess immense power to shape the world, capable of both destruction and salvation. He highlights that our daily choices, not just grand actions, influence global outcomes. The actor's quote urges introspection on how we wield this power and responsibility, stressing conscious action over fear of the future.

articles.title: West Bengal elections: TMC alleges strongroom breaches, BJP mocks claim
articles.description: Trinamool Congress has raised concerns with the Election Commission regarding alleged CCTV blackouts and unauthorized access to EVM strongrooms. BJP dismissed these claims as tactics by a party anticipating defeat. TMC representatives reported seeing individuals inside sealed rooms without party agents present, fueling suspicion ahead of counting day.
